element_introduction_order.mastery_context: All three characters start with foundational combat moves
Defeating enemies awards XP; stronger finishing moves grant more points
Simple fodder enemies teach basic combat rhythm
Chibi-styled boss with interactive dialogue prompts that damage him if answered correctly
Cody gets knife, Guy gets shurikens, Haggar gets mallet; drop from defeated enemies
Vitality restoration, extra lives, continue items scattered throughout levels
Tornado Sweep (Cody), Tornado Kick (Guy), Running Clothesline (Haggar); performed via directional pad + attack button
Japanese version includes true/false trivia quiz for extra continue; Western versions removed this feature
Optional challenge rounds against childlike counterparts of Mad Gear members
Mechanically enhanced version of arcade antagonist; requires mastery of all learned techniques

typical_relief_triggers: Leveling up (stat increases provide safety buffer)
typical_tension_triggers: New enemy types introduced in later stages
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Steady escalation with character progression gates. Early stages establish mechanics through weak enemies and frequent XP rewards. Mid-stages introduce boss patterns and special moves. Late stages compress challenge through enemy density and Cyborg Belger's mechanical complexity. Leveling system provides consistent tension relief as player power scales.

difficulty_curve.early_game: Tutorial-adjacent. Stage 1 introduces weak minions and Thrasher boss with interactive dialogue mechanic. Cody and Guy start at skill level 1; Haggar at level 3. XP gains frequent to encourage engagement with finishing move system.
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Stages 2-3 introduce weapons and enemies with varied attack patterns. Player typically reaches skill level 4 by Stage 2-3, unlocking character-specific special moves (Tornado Sweep, Tornado Kick, Running Clothesline). Boss encounters (Masamune, Abigail) require pattern recognition and special move execution.
difficulty_curve.late_game: Stages 4-5 compress enemy encounters and introduce Murasame boss, then final Cyborg Belger. Cyborg Belger represents damage output spike requiring full mastery of learned techniques and optimal stat allocation via leveling.
notable_spikes: Cyborg Belger (Stage 5): Mechanically enhanced form of main antagonist; requires mastery of all combat systems
Abigail (Stage 3): Japanese version includes quiz mechanic; significantly harder if player cannot answer trivia correctly

notable_level_patterns.description: Each stage advances horizontally, populated with progressively harder enemy waves. Weak minions appear frequently; bosses punctuate stage endings.
New mechanics and upgrades gate behind boss encounters. Interactive dialogue in Thrasher fight, weapons after stage 1, special moves unlock at skill level 4 during stages 2-3.
Optional challenge rounds between main stages serve as mechanical breather and reward accumulation opportunity without advancing primary story.
Stat scaling (health, attack power) directly correlates to finishing move variety and power. Haggar's early level advantage creates asymmetric starting conditions for three-character system.

sources_used: tcrf (Mighty Final Fight - The Cutting Room Floor)
